children.heading: Appeal of denial of application for removal proceedings
children.label: a Appeal of denial of application for removal proceedings

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Attorney General may seek a review of the denial of an order sought in an application filed pursuant to . The appeal shall be filed in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit by notice of appeal filed not later than 20 days after the date of such denial.section 1533 of this title

children.heading: Record on appeal
children.label: 2 Record on appeal
children.text: The entire record of the proceeding shall be transmitted to the Court of Appeals under seal, and the Court of Appeals shall hear the matter ex parte.

children.label: A
children.text: review questions of law de novo; and

children.label: B
children.text: set aside a finding of fact only if such finding was clearly erroneous.

children.heading: Appeal of determination regarding summary of classified information
children.label: b Appeal of determination regarding summary of classified information
A
children.text: any determination by the judge pursuant to ; orsection 1534(e)(3) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the refusal of the court to make the findings permitted by .section 1534(e)(3) of this title

children.heading: Record
children.label: 2 Record
children.text: In any interlocutory appeal taken pursuant to this subsection, the entire record, including any proposed order of the judge, any classified information and the summary of evidence, shall be transmitted to the Court of Appeals. The classified information shall be transmitted under seal. A verbatim record of such appeal shall be kept under seal in the event of any other judicial review.

children.heading: Appeal of decision in hearing
children.label: c Appeal of decision in hearing

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the decision of the judge after a removal hearing may be appealed by either the alien or the Attorney General to the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit by notice of appeal filed not later than 20 days after the date on which the order is issued. The order shall not be enforced during the pendency of an appeal under this subsection.

children.heading: Automatic appeals in cases of permanent resident aliens in which no summary provided
children.label: 2 Automatic appeals in cases of permanent resident aliens in which no summary provided

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Unless the alien waives the right to a review under this paragraph, in any case involving an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence who is denied a written summary of classified information under and with respect to which the procedures described in apply, any order issued by the judge shall be reviewed by the Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.section 1534(e)(3) of this titlesection 1534(e)(3)(F) of this title

children.heading: Use of special attorney
children.label: B Use of special attorney
children.text: With respect to any issue relating to classified information that arises in such review, the alien shall be represented only by the special attorney designated under on behalf of the alien.section 1534(e)(3)(F)(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the entire record shall be transmitted to the Court of Appeals; and

children.label: B
children.text: information received in camera and ex parte, and any portion of the order that would reveal the substance or source of such information, shall be transmitted under seal.

children.label: A
children.text: the appeal or review shall be heard as expeditiously as practicable and the court may dispense with full briefing and hear the matter solely on the record of the judge of the removal court and on such briefs or motions as the court may require to be filed by the parties;

children.label: B
children.text: the Court of Appeals shall issue an opinion not later than 60 days after the date of the issuance of the final order of the district court;

children.label: C
children.text: the court shall review all questions of law de novo; and

children.label: D
children.text: a finding of fact shall be accorded deference by the reviewing court and shall not be set aside unless such finding was clearly erroneous, except that in the case of a review under paragraph (2) in which an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence was denied a written summary of classified information under section 1534(c)(3) of this title, the Court of Appeals shall review questions of fact de novo.1So in original. Probably should be section “1534(e)(3)”.1

children.heading: Certiorari
children.label: d Certiorari
children.text: Following a decision by the Court of Appeals pursuant to subsection (c), the alien or the Attorney General may petition the Supreme Court for a writ of certiorari. In any such case, any information transmitted to the Court of Appeals under seal shall, if such information is also submitted to the Supreme Court, be transmitted under seal. Any order of removal shall not be stayed pending disposition of a writ of certiorari, except as provided by the Court of Appeals or a Justice of the Supreme Court.

children.label: A
children.text: for purposes of section 3145 of such title an appeal shall be taken to the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit; and

children.label: B
children.text: for purposes of section 3146 of such title the alien shall be considered released in connection with a charge of an offense punishable by life imprisonment.

children.heading: No review of continued detention
children.label: 2 No review of continued detention
children.text: The determinations and actions of the Attorney General pursuant to shall not be subject to judicial review, including application for a writ of habeas corpus, except for a claim by the alien that continued detention violates the alien’s rights under the Constitution. Jurisdiction over any such challenge shall lie exclusively in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.section 1537(b)(2)(C) of this title
